stem: A car rental company uses the function $P(d) = 18d + 75$ to determine the total price, in dollars, of renting a car for $d$ days. What does the value 75 represent in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The daily rental rate, in dollars per day

choices.label: B
choices.text: The total cost of renting the car for 75 days

choices.label: C
choices.text: The total cost of renting the car for 1 day

choices.label: D
choices.text: The one-time fee charged at the start of any rental, regardless of the number of days

explanation: In the linear function $P(d) = 18d + 75$, the constant term $75$ is the y-intercept. When $d = 0$ (no days rented), $P(0) = 18(0) + 75 = 75$. This means $\$75$ is the amount owed before any days are counted — a one-time fixed fee at the start of the rental. The coefficient $18$ is the slope, representing the $\$18$ daily rate.

distractorRationale.A: Confuses the y-intercept (75) with the slope (18); the daily rate is the coefficient of $d$, which is 18, not 75.
distractorRationale.B: Misreads the constant 75 as an input value (number of days) rather than an output value (price component), imagining a 75-day rental scenario.
distractorRationale.C: Evaluates $P(1) = 18(1) + 75 = 93$ is the 1-day cost, but the distractor error is: the student reads 75 as the total 1-day cost, ignoring the daily charge component and failing to add $18 \times 1 = 18$.
